Cimicifuga, or Black Cohosh, is a perennial plant native to North America, known for its erect stems and white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the alleviation of symptoms associated with menopause, such as hot flashes and mood changes. Bioactive constituents of Cimicifuga include triterpenoid saponins, including actein and 23-epi-26-deoxycucurbitacin E, which are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cimicifuga include capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to support women's health and relieve symptoms of menopause.
